title: CPU usage issue with aardvark-dns on Fedora 35 running podman 4.0.0-rc4
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. Create a Vagrant machine on with KVM hypervisor using the following Vagrantfile:
```
Vagrant.configure("2") do |config|
  config.vm.box = "fedora/35-cloud-base"
  config.vm.hostname = "f35.example.com"
  config.vm.provider "libvirt" do |lv|
    lv.memory = 8192
    lv.cpus = 2
  end
end
```

2. Install Podman 4.0.0-rc4 from rhcontainerbot/podman4 copr repositories.
```
sudo dnf copr enable rhcontainerbot/podman4
sudo dnf install podman
```
If Podman is already installed upgrade to the latest release candidate:
```
sudo dnf upgrade podman
```

If CNI network was previously used it is necessary to run a `podman system reset` to clean up all previsou configurations and start using netavark and aadvark-dns.

3. Create a new network:
```
sudo podman network create test-net1
```

4. Create a test container:
```
sudo podman run -d --net test-net1 --name test-c1 docker.io/library/httpd:latest
```

After the container is started the aardvark-dns process is started to serve the network resolution for the `test-net` network. Aardvark stores its configuration for rootful containers under `/run/containers/networks/aardvark-dns`.

**Describe the results you received:**
The container networking seems to work correctly but the aardvark-dns process eats up to 100% of CPU on a dual thread Vagrant virtual machine running with KVM hypervisor.

```
$ top -b -p $(pgrep aardvark-dns) 1
top - 23:33:17 up  4:26,  1 user,  load average: 2.68, 2.76, 2.11
Tasks:   1 total,   0 running,   1 sleeping,   0 stopped,   0 zombie
%Cpu0  :100.0 us,  0.0 sy,  0.0 ni,  0.0 id,  0.0 wa,  0.0 hi,  0.0 si,  0.0 st     %Cpu1  :  0.0 us,  0.0 sy,  0.0 ni,100.0 id,  0.0 wa,  0.0 hi,  0.0 si,  0.0 st
MiB Mem :   7940.0 total,   6180.4 free,    226.0 used,   1533.6 buff/cache
MiB Swap:   7940.0 total,   7940.0 free,      0.0 used.   7446.3 avail Mem 

    PID USER      PR  NI    VIRT    RES    SHR S  %CPU  %MEM     TIME+ COMMAND
  14596 root      20   0  682728   2660   1972 S 100.0   0.0  65:26.18 aardvark-dns
```

Adding more networks and containers the resulting CPU time increases up to the CPU saturation limit.


**Describe the results you expected:**
The aardvark-dns process runs within acceptable CPU comsuption rates.


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Issue happens constantly.

The issue was already opened and closed on aardvark-dns project.
https://github.com/containers/aardvark-dns/issues/46

The following PR has fixed the issue but this is not yet implemented in the current aardvark-dns package installed with the Podman release in Fedora:
https://github.com/containers/aardvark-dns/pull/48

**Output of `podman version`:**
```
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.0.0-rc4
API Version:  4.0.0-rc4
Go Version:   go1.16.13

Built:      Fri Feb  4 15:25:35 2022
OS/Arch:    linux/amd64
```

**Output of `/usr/libexec/podman/aardvark-dns --version`:**
```
/usr/libexec/podman/aardvark-dns --version
aardvark-dns 1.0.0-rc1
```


**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.24.0
  cgroupControllers:
  - memory
  - pids
  cgroupManager: systemd
  cgroupVersion: v2
  conmon:
    package: conmon-2.1.0-2.fc35.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.1.0, commit: '
  cpus: 2
  distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    variant: cloud
    version: "35"
  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: f35.example.com
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1000
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 100000
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.14.10-300.fc35.x86_64
  linkmode: dynamic
  logDriver: journald
  memFree: 6475481088
  memTotal: 8325742592
  networkBackend: netavark
  ociRuntime:
    name: crun
    package: crun-1.4.2-1.fc35.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/crun
    version: |-
      crun version 1.4.2
      commit: f6fbc8f840df1a414f31a60953ae514fa497c748
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +CRIU +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: true
    seccompEnabled: true
    seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json
    selinuxEnabled: true
  serviceIsRemote: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: /usr/bin/slirp4netns
    package: slirp4netns-1.1.12-2.fc35.x86_64
    version: |-
      slirp4netns version 1.1.12
      commit: 7a104a101aa3278a2152351a082a6df71f57c9a3
      libslirp: 4.6.1
      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 3
      libseccomp: 2.5.0
  swapFree: 8325689344
  swapTotal: 8325689344
  uptime: 4h 27m 45.9s (Approximately 0.17 days)
plugins:
  log:
  - k8s-file
  - none
  - passthrough
  - journald
  network:
  - bridge
  - macvlan
  volume:
  - local
registries:
  search:
  - registry.fedoraproject.org
  - registry.access.redhat.com
  - docker.io
  - quay.io
store:
  configFile: /home/vagrant/.config/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 2
    paused: 0
    running: 0
    stopped: 2
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions: {}
  graphRoot: /home/vagrant/.local/share/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: btrfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "true"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  imageCopyTmpDir: /var/tmp
  imageStore:
    number: 2
  runRoot: /run/user/1000/containers
  volumePath: /home/vagrant/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 4.0.0-rc4
  Built: 1643988335
  BuiltTime: Fri Feb  4 15:25:35 2022
  GitCommit: ""
  GoVersion: go1.16.13
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 4.0.0-rc4
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
rpm -q podman
podman-4.0.0-0.5.rc4.fc35.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Vagrant with LIbvirt KVM Provider
VM OS: Fedora 35
Host OS: Fedora 34

comments.body:
Tested a new build of aardvark-dns with the merged PR https://github.com/containers/aardvark-dns/pull/48 and the issue appears to be solved.

**Steps to reproduce:**
1. Clone the aardvark-dns repository
```
git clone https://github.com/containers/aardvark-dns.git
```

2. Install necessary dependecies to build
```
sudo dnf install -y make rust cargo
```

3. Build the new binary
```
cd aardvark-dns
make
```

4. Copy the new binary under /usr/libexec/podman
```
cp ./bin/aardvark-dns /usr/libexec/podman
```

5. Test by running a new container
```
sudo podman run -d --net test-net1 --name test-c1 docker.io/library/httpd:latest
```

**Expected results:**
CPU compusumption should be minimal for the aardvark-dns process.
```
top -b -p $(pgrep aardvark-dns)
top - 00:14:24 up  5:07,  1 user,  load average: 0.00, 0.35, 0.91
Tasks:   1 total,   0 running,   1 sleeping,   0 stopped,   0 zombie
%Cpu(s):  0.0 us,  0.0 sy,  0.0 ni,100.0 id,  0.0 wa,  0.0 hi,  0.0 si,  0.0 st
MiB Mem :   7940.0 total,   5214.3 free,    237.9 used,   2487.9 buff/cache
MiB Swap:   7940.0 total,   7940.0 free,      0.0 used.   7419.6 avail Mem 

    PID USER      PR  NI    VIRT    RES    SHR S  %CPU  %MEM     TIME+ COMMAND
  22162 root      20   0  479944   2436   2016 S   0.0   0.0   0:00.00 aardvark-dns
  ```

> Tested a new build of aardvark-dns with the merged PR [containers/aardvark-dns#48](https://github.com/containers/aardvark-dns/pull/48) and the issue appears to be solved.
> 
> **Steps to reproduce:**
> 
>     1. Clone the aardvark-dns repository
> 
> 
> ```
> git clone https://github.com/containers/aardvark-dns.git
> ```
> 
>     2. Install necessary dependecies to build
> 
> 
> ```
> sudo dnf install -y make rust cargo
> ```
> 
>     3. Build the new binary
> 
> 
> ```
> cd aardvark-dns
> make
> ```
> 
>     4. Copy the new binary under /usr/libexec/podman
> 
> 
> ```
> cp ./bin/aardvark-dns /usr/libexec/podman
> ```
> 
>     5. Test by running a new container
> 
> 
> ```
> sudo podman run -d --net test-net1 --name test-c1 docker.io/library/httpd:latest
> ```
> 
> **Expected results:** CPU compusumption should be minimal for the aardvark-dns process.
> 
> ```
> top -b -p $(pgrep aardvark-dns)
> top - 00:14:24 up  5:07,  1 user,  load average: 0.00, 0.35, 0.91
> Tasks:   1 total,   0 running,   1 sleeping,   0 stopped,   0 zombie
> %Cpu(s):  0.0 us,  0.0 sy,  0.0 ni,100.0 id,  0.0 wa,  0.0 hi,  0.0 si,  0.0 st
> MiB Mem :   7940.0 total,   5214.3 free,    237.9 used,   2487.9 buff/cache
> MiB Swap:   7940.0 total,   7940.0 free,      0.0 used.   7419.6 avail Mem 
> 
>     PID USER      PR  NI    VIRT    RES    SHR S  %CPU  %MEM     TIME+ COMMAND
>   22162 root      20   0  479944   2436   2016 S   0.0   0.0   0:00.00 aardvark-dns
> ```

Yes this should not be in upstream anymore. But its weird that you had to manually pull and build from upstream.

@baude @lsm5 is copr not reflecting upstream commit for aardvark.
I'm closing this issue since copr reflecting latest upstream is a different issue.
